claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 20 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 20, which is 20 or over
circumstances: Child is 20, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility criteria, and then I'll ask you some questions about your situation.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. I've reviewed the eligibility rules. To help determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit, I need to ask you a few questions about your situation.

First, do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have one child. His name is Alex, and I'm wondering if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! I'd like to help you determine if you can claim Child Benefit for Alex.

To start, how old is Alex?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 20 years old, I need to ask: Is Alex currently in any approved education or training?

(Child Benefit can continue for children under 20 if they're in approved education or training, but stops once they turn 20.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Based on what you've told me, I now have enough information to assess Alex's eligibility for Child Benefit.

Let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Assessment Result:**

I'm afraid you are **not eligible** to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

**The reason is:** Alex is 20 years old, which exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit. According to the eligibility rules:
- Child Benefit is normally available for children under 16
- For children aged 16 or over, you can still qualify if they are under 20 AND in approved education or training

Since Alex has turned 20, he is beyond the age limit for Child Benefit, regardless of whether he is in education or training.

If you have any other questions or if your circumstances change, please feel free to ask.

reasonings: Alex is 20 years old and is not currently in approved education or training. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 20 years old, he has exceeded the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility, even if he were in approved education or training. Therefore, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 20 years old and not in education or training. Child Benefit eligibility ends when a child turns 20, or earlier if they are 16-19 and not in approved education or training. Since Alex is 20, he exceeds the age limit and the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.

According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 20 years old, he has exceeded the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility, even if he were in approved education or training. Therefore, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 20 years old and not in education or training. Child Benefit eligibility ends when a child turns 20, or earlier if they are 16-19 and not in approved education or training. Since Alex is 20, he exceeds the age limit and the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.
